title: chrysanthemum leucanthemum subsp. controversum
intro: Chrysanthemum leucanthemum subsp. controversum, also known as the Corn Marigold or Cornflower Chrysanthemum, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is believed to have medicinal properties such as soothing digestive issues and reducing fever. The health benefits of Chrysanthemum leucanthemum subsp. controversum include its ability to act as an antiseptic, antispasmodic, and diuretic, making it useful for treating a variety of health issues. Chrysanthemum leucanthemum subsp. controversum can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
